&C. h. Boyles, 1923 jackson ave-
nue., will build the concrete bridges
and culverts on the North Coast
Tor the first 20 miles between Spo-
Nane and Tekoa on the Palouse
branch, for consideration of $685.
goo, Twenty-three of the Al miles
OF grade between Spokane and Te-
koa have been awarded to Spokane
contractors. Grading between Spo-
kane and Tiona has also begun,
with seven camps along the line.
Forty-two deeds and 30 mort
cages were filed for record yester-
day at the auditors office. The
mortgages are mostly on city prop
erty and vary in size from 500 to
$4,000. The comparatively large
ratio of loans as compared with
the transfers indicates that money
is easy.


Companies H and ! of the WASH
ington National guard have taken
on new life and are now entering
into busy winters training.
Twelve new men have recently
been added to- the companies.
| TWenty-four parcels of state
school lands will be offered for sale
Saturday by County Auditor But
Ter. Most of it is unimproved
farming or timber land, while there
are some lots in the school section
of Spokane


The adoption Of odd lengths in
the various standard grades of
lumber was advocated in an ad
dress before the Western Pine
Manufacturers association yester-
day by V. H. Beckman, delivered
at the meeting held in the assem-
bly, room of the chamber of COM
merce. This plan is being gener
ally adopted on the coast, as it
means a saving both to the manu-
facturer and the consumer. At pres
ent the standard grades of lumber
are cut only in even lengths.


c. L. Smith, industrial agent of
the lnalnd Empire system, is be-
lever in dry farming and insists
that 10 acres under the plan of
intensified farming will produce as
much as 80 or 100 acres under the
one crop plan. He thinks the COM
ing of the Dry Farming congress
next year can be made as important
an event as was the irrigation con.
gress this year.


To the Mystic Order Of E-Nak-
Ops will be given the honor of open
ing the Apple Show November IS.
One thousand members of the or
ganization are expected to turn out
on that occasion and set the ball
In motion with one long, loud,
scream of joy. A street parade by
the E-Nak-Ops, headed by the con-
solidated Apple Show band of SO
pieces will precede the night per-
Torrance. The parade will be a
feature worth seeing, as many
novel ideas will be exploited.


A. D. Charlton, general passenger
agent of the Northern Pacific, while
in the city, said that his company
is sending out million circulars
about the National Apple show, and
has inaugurated an advertising
campaign for the show that equals
that waged for the A. Y. P.


Statistics gathered from reliable
Esou5ces show that the apple pro-
Edudion of the world has decreased
Fin IA years from 60,000,000 barrels
to 27,0OO,000 in 1909. The apple
orchards of the vast Mississippi
VALLEY, which furnished the bulk of
1.-The crop years ago, have been sadly
neglected, and many of them are
dying out.
